title: Lewis University Library AI Vibe Code Repository — Project Schema
description: Canonical metadata schema for one project in the Lewis University Library AI Vibe Code Repository. Each record describes a browser-based, AI-assisted interactive application and documents its purpose, contributors, AI-assisted build process, source pathway, publication status, and discovery metadata.

id.description: URL-safe unique identifier, ideally matching the GitHub repository slug.

title.description: Public title shown in the repository gallery.

description.description: Card-ready description of what the application does, who it serves, and why it matters.

department.description: Lewis University department, office, college, or unit connected to the project.

disciplines.description: Disciplinary tags used for filtering and discovery.

ai_tools.description: AI tools or coding assistants used in the build process.

format.description: The repository is scoped to interactive browser-based applications.

content_type.description: Application type shown as the gallery category.

blooms_level.description: Primary Bloom's Taxonomy level for instructional apps.

date_submitted.description: Date submitted for repository review, formatted YYYY-MM-DD.

live_url.description: Public URL for the deployed application.

source_url.description: Public source repository or code archive URL.

status.description: Publication workflow state. Only published projects should appear in the public gallery.

interdisciplinary.description: Whether the project intentionally crosses disciplines, departments, or user groups.

course.description: Course number/name if the app was built for a specific course.

license.description: Source or content license selected by the contributor.

purpose.description: Pedagogical, research, service, or workflow need the application addresses.

ai_role.description: Narrative description of how AI assisted the build and how human expertise shaped the result.

human_review.description: Human review, testing, or validation completed before submission.

accessibility_notes.description: Accessibility considerations, checks, or known limitations.

screenshot.description: Relative path or URL to a project preview image.

maintenance_contact.description: Optional contact person/team for updates or broken links.
